details.body: In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs` around lines 1407-1449, the PromptReview resume path does NOT handle `BackendExhausted` during `check_availability` — exhausted validators are treated the same as other errors (fail if required, silently drop if optional). This is inconsistent with the CompletionPanel and FinalReview resume paths (lines 1453-1553) which both skip BackendExhausted members gracefully with quorum reduction. The execution-time probe for PromptReview (line ~6486) DOES handle BackendExhausted correctly, so the inconsistency only affects the resume codepath. A required prompt-review validator that is BackendExhausted during resume will cause a hard ResumeDriftFailure instead of graceful degradation.

Narrow stderr before calling `is_backend_exhausted` in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1401-1420`, `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1443-1455`, and `src/adapters/tmux.rs:632-643`. Right now `classify_exit_failure_with_output` receives the full stderr, and `is_backend_exhausted` does raw substring matches over that full text in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1768-1774`. Codex stderr includes the echoed user prompt before the final error line (for example `.ralph-burning/projects/9ni-2-4/runtime/failed/run-20260327233800-final_review-reviewer-reviewer-2-c2-a1-cr10.failed.raw:14-186`), so any unrelated non-zero exit will be misclassified as `BackendExhausted` whenever the prompt itself quotes "usage limit", "quota exceeded", etc. Extract or scan only the terminal error portion of stderr, the same way stdout is narrowed, before making the non-retryable classification.

Either remove the new `BackendExhausted` degradation from prompt-review, or finish it end-to-end. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6487-6536` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6607-6618` now reduce prompt-review quorum when validators are exhausted and pass the reduced minimum into execution, but `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:174-195` still propagates any validator invocation error with `?`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:241-247` has no exhausted-member handling at all. A validator that passes availability probing and then exhausts during the actual prompt-review call will still fail the entire stage, so the new engine behavior is inconsistent and only half implemented.

Preserve probe-time exhaustion information when building completion/final-review aggregates. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6824-6847` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7048-7101` drop exhausted members before execution and compute a reduced minimum; `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6906-6917` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7160-7173` then pass only that reduced minimum into the executors. The aggregate writers in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:279-287`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:534-555`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1415-1496` therefore record `min_completers`/`effective_min_reviewers` and `exhausted_count` using only post-snapshot data, even though `src/contexts/workflow_composition/panel_contracts.rs:116-124` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/panel_contracts.rs:230-236` document those fields as the configured minimum plus exhaustion count. After a probe-time skip, the persisted aggregate can no longer tell that any backend was exhausted, and `CompletionAggregatePayload.min_completers` is no longer the configured value it claims to be.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: Fix the completion panel's early quorum check in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:195-197` to include `probe_exhausted_count`. The final quorum calculation at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:256-257` subtracts both probe-time and invoke-time exhaustion, but the early-break path subtracts only `total_exhausted_count`. With `min_completers=3`, one probe-time exhaustion, and one invoke-time exhaustion, this code aborts even though the last completer should still satisfy the degraded quorum of 1.

Apply `effective_min_override` to prompt-review drift validation as well. The resume path now computes and stores a reduced prompt-review quorum when validators are exhausted (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:1475-1478`), but `drift_still_satisfies_requirements()` still hard-codes the configured `min_reviewers` for `StageId::PromptReview` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7586-7597`). A failed prompt-review run that is resumed after an exhausted validator can therefore be rejected on drift even when the reduced quorum is satisfied.

When reconciling a stale running snapshot from the journal, update `status_summary` along with `status`. Both reconciliation sites only flip `status` and clear `active_run` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/queries.rs:29-45`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:1301-1310`), so `run status` can show `failed` or `completed` while the summary still says `running: ...`, and the resume path can persist that contradictory snapshot.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs` fail_run() (line ~5553): `std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100))` blocks the tokio worker thread. Replace with `tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await` since `fail_run` is already an async function. While 100ms is short and this is a failure-only path, blocking an async executor thread is a correctness issue — under high concurrency it could delay other tasks on the same thread.

Keep the configured quorum when exhausted members were only excess/optional slots. In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6880-6894` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:197-199,260-283`, and again in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7135-7149` plus `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:296-299,334-336,909-913,1133-1135`, the code computes `effective_min = min - exhausted`. That is only correct when the panel had no slack. For a panel with 3 members and `min=2`, exhausting the optional 3rd member currently lowers the threshold to 1, so a single completer/reviewer can decide the stage even though the original minimum is still satisfiable with the 2 remaining members. The effective minimum needs to stay at the configured minimum until exhaustion actually removes the last available slack slot.

Handle stdout error envelopes in the success-exit path of `src/adapters/process_backend.rs`. The new exhaustion classifier only runs on non-zero exits (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1418-1480`), but `finish()` for Claude still ignores `is_error` envelopes on a zero exit (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:145-205`). Because `extract_stdout_error` already recognizes that envelope format (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1614-1636`), a backend that returns `{"is_error":true,"result":"You've hit your usage limit"}` while exiting 0 will still be turned into a `SchemaValidationFailure` retry instead of `BackendExhausted`, so the run will not degrade gracefully for that backend.

In prompt_review.rs, the BackendExhausted handler (lines 208-216) only calls tracing::warn — it does not write a journal event (append_panel_member_completed_event) or a runtime log entry (append_runtime_log). In contrast, final_review.rs writes both a journal event with outcome 'failed_exhausted' AND a runtime log entry, and completion.rs writes a runtime log entry. This observability gap means prompt_review exhaustion events are invisible in the journal audit trail and runtime logs, making post-mortem debugging harder. Add journal event and runtime log writes to the prompt_review exhaustion handler, consistent with the other two panels.

There are no tests for prompt_review panel degradation when a validator fails with BackendExhausted. Both final_review (final_review_degrades_when_one_reviewer_backend_exhausted, final_review_fails_when_all_reviewers_exhausted) and completion (completion_degrades_when_one_completer_exhausted, completion_fails_when_all_completers_exhausted) have paired degradation tests. Add equivalent tests for prompt_review to verify: (1) panel succeeds when one validator is exhausted and another succeeds, and (2) panel fails when all validators are exhausted.

In `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:157-171`, stop looking only at `envelope.result` when an exit-0 Claude envelope has `is_error: true`. `extract_stdout_error` in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1632-1650` already knows how to pull error text from `result`, `errors[]`, and `subtype`, but the new exhaustion path bypasses that helper. As written, an exit-0 envelope like `{"is_error":true,"errors":["quota exceeded"]}` or a subtype-only exhaustion error will fall through into normal payload parsing, become a schema-validation failure, and still retry instead of degrading gracefully.

Broaden the persistent-rate-limit detection in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1842-1848`. The current implementation only upgrades to `BackendExhausted` when the text contains both `rate limit` and the exact phrase `try again at`. Reset-time variants such as `retry after 2h`, `until 2026-04-06T15:03Z`, or other timestamp/duration wordings will still be classified as `TransportFailure` and retried.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:205-220` and `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:345-377` only upgrade exhaustion to `FailureClass::BackendExhausted` for HTTP 429. Any credit/billing exhaustion that comes back as another 4xx (for example 402/403 with quota text in the body) still falls through to retryable `TransportFailure`/generic `BackendUnavailable`, so the run will keep retrying instead of degrading gracefully. The body-pattern check should run before the status-specific split, or at least cover the other billing/quota statuses in both `send_probe` and `map_http_error`.

In final_review.rs, the post-loop quorum checks (line ~541 for proposals, line ~1150 for votes) return InsufficientPanelMembers without propagating a BackendExhausted failure class, unlike the equivalent checks in prompt_review.rs (line ~292) and completion.rs (line ~267) which use a last_exhaustion_error pattern. In practice the early-exit within the loop always catches the all-exhausted case, so this is not currently reachable as a bug. However, for defensive consistency, final_review.rs should adopt the same last_exhaustion_error propagation pattern so that any future refactoring that changes the early-exit logic doesn't silently degrade the failure classification.

In openrouter_backend.rs check_availability (line ~195), the probe targets /models/<model_id> which is a model metadata endpoint. Quota/billing exhaustion errors typically surface on /chat/completions, not /models. The is_backend_exhausted check on the probe response body may therefore never fire for real quota exhaustion. Consider either (a) documenting this as a known limitation in a code comment, or (b) adding a lightweight completions probe (e.g. a minimal request with max_tokens=1) as an alternative availability check when the models endpoint succeeds but the account may be quota-limited.

Expand the persistent-rate-limit matcher in [src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1859] and [src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1877]. Right now `is_backend_exhausted` only treats `retry after` values as persistent when the suffix starts with `h`/`hour`/`day`/`min`/`week`, and the absolute-time branch only looks for a few hard-coded phrases like `try again at` or `until 2`. Common reset messages such as `retry after 3600s`, `retry after 86400 seconds`, or `until 9:00 PM UTC` still fall through to `TransportFailure`, so the system will keep burning retries on the long-lived limits this feature is supposed to skip.

Stop using raw stdout prefixes as exhaustion evidence on non-zero exits, or make that path much stricter. In [src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1495] and [src/adapters/tmux.rs:638], the adapter falls back to the first 4 KB of stdout and feeds it into `is_backend_exhausted` ([src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1840]). If a backend has already emitted partial model output containing phrases like `usage limit` or `quota exceeded`, a transient transport failure will be misclassified as `BackendExhausted`, which disables retries and can incorrectly drop that backend from the panel.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: In prompt_review.rs exhaustion handler (around line 210-231), unlike completion.rs and final_review.rs, there is no early-exit quorum check when a validator is exhausted. The handler just does `continue` without checking if `executed_count + remaining < effective_min`. This means all remaining validators will be invoked even when it's mathematically impossible to reach quorum. Add the same early-exit pattern used in the other two panels: compute effective_min inside the exhaustion branch and `break` if `executed_count + panel.validators.len().saturating_sub(i + 1) < effective_min`.

The `is_backend_exhausted` function's "retry after" duration parser does not handle unitless large numbers (e.g., "retry after 3600" with no unit suffix). The numeric_end extraction works, but `rest` is empty (or non-unit text), so none of the unit checks (h, hour, day, min, sec, s) match. Consider treating large unitless numbers (>= 3600) as persistent, or at minimum add a test documenting this as a known gap.

`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1442-1463` and `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1485-1495` only pass `extract_stdout_error()` into `classify_exit_failure_with_output`, and `src/adapters/tmux.rs:632-643` does the same. That means a backend that exits non-zero and prints a plain stdout error like `usage limit` / `quota exceeded` without a JSON envelope is still classified as `TransportFailure`, so the run keeps retrying instead of degrading. The helper itself explicitly allows raw stdout fallback when no structured error is available (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1822-1825`), but the call sites never do that.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:172-209` now skips exhausted validators and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:285-295` lowers the quorum, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6550-6612` removes exhausted validators before the prompt-review snapshot is persisted. The feature request only called for graceful degradation in `final_review` and `completion`; applying the same rule to `prompt_review` weakens the prompt-contract safety gate and lets a refined prompt be accepted with fewer validators than the configured `min_reviewers`.

Tighten the new rate-limit exhaustion heuristic before shipping it. `is_backend_exhausted` currently upgrades any `rate limit` message with `retry after 60 sec` (and other broad `until <digit>` forms) to `BackendExhausted` in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1865-1903`, and `openrouter_backend::map_http_error` consumes that helper before falling back to retryable 429 handling in `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:349-370`. That means a normal transient 429 with a one-minute reset window will now bypass retries and permanently drop the backend, which violates the requirement to preserve retry behavior for genuinely transient errors.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: In `src/adapters/process_backend.rs`, the `is_backend_exhausted` function's "retry after" duration parser does not recognize the common shorthand `m` for minutes (e.g., "retry after 30m"). The `rest.starts_with("min")` check requires at least three characters, so `"m"` alone doesn't match. Adding `rest.starts_with('m') && !rest.starts_with("ms")` would close this gap without introducing false positives for milliseconds.

In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs`, the `_probe_exhausted_count` parameter (line 78) is accepted but unused (underscore-prefixed). Unlike final_review and completion which pass `probe_exhausted_count` through to their aggregate payloads for observability, prompt_review silently discards it. This means operators cannot distinguish between probe-time and invocation-time exhaustion in prompt_review logs/artifacts.

Fix the effective-min quorum calculation so it is based on the post-optional-removal participant set, not the original panel length. In src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:434-519, 1498-1535, 1573-1608, 6865-6903, and 7125-7164, `effective_min` is computed from `members.len()` / `panel.*.len()` minus only the exhausted members. That breaks the new degradation path in mixed cases: if an optional reviewer/completer is already omitted for an ordinary availability failure and another remaining backend is `BackendExhausted`, the stage still fails even though the feature is supposed to continue with the remaining available reviewer(s)/completer(s). Base the reduction on `available + exhausted` (the members that would have participated absent exhaustion), and apply the same fix in preflight and resume.

details.message: stage commit failed for stage 'final_review': max completion rounds exceeded: 21/20

details.body: In final_review.rs, the early-exit quorum check in the optional-failure arm (lines 382-394) propagates the optional member's non-exhaustion error via `return Err(error)` when remaining members can't reach quorum. This means an optional reviewer's transient failure (e.g. timeout) can abort the entire panel even though another optional reviewer exhausting is what made quorum impossible. Consider propagating a more descriptive InsufficientPanelMembers error instead, or at minimum logging which combination of failures (optional + exhaustion) caused the quorum shortfall, so operators can distinguish 'panel had too few members' from 'an optional member timed out'.

The `is_backend_exhausted` function's 'until <time-of-day>' check (process_backend.rs ~line 1903-1908) matches any digit after 'until ', which would incorrectly trigger on hypothetical messages like 'Rate limit, until 5 retries are completed' or 'Rate limit exceeded until 3 requests are freed'. Consider tightening the regex to require a colon after the digit (time-of-day format like '9:00') or an AM/PM marker, to avoid matching non-temporal 'until <number>' phrases.

src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6579-6597, 6881-6900, 7134-7152, 1477-1608: preserve the configured quorum for non-exhaustion availability failures. The new `effective_min_* = min(configured_min, available.len()).max(1)` calculation runs after optional members are dropped for any failed availability probe, not just `BackendExhausted`. That makes the `available.len() < effective_min_*` guard false whenever at least one member survives, so a plain optional `BackendUnavailable`/`BinaryNotFound` now silently relaxes completion/final-review (and prompt-review/resume) to a single participant. Only members skipped specifically for `BackendExhausted` should lower quorum; other probe-time unavailability should still enforce the original minimum or fail with `InsufficientPanelMembers`.

src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:180-188, 315-329 and src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6548-6599: remove the new prompt-review degradation path, or gate it behind an explicit policy flag. The bead only called for graceful degradation in `completion` and `final_review`, but this change also lets `prompt_review` accept a refined prompt with fewer validators whenever one backend exhausts credits.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1875-1882 classifies any `rate limit ... retry after <n>m` / `minutes` message as `BackendExhausted`, and src/adapters/process_backend.rs:3750-3763 bakes in `retry after 2m` as a terminal case. Via src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1938-1950 that becomes non-retryable, so normal minute-scale 429s will now be skipped or fail a panel instead of using the existing retry policy. This breaks the "do not break existing retry behavior for genuinely transient errors" requirement; short minute-based backoffs should stay `TransportFailure` unless the wait is clearly long/persistent.

src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1639-1649 short-circuits `extract_stdout_error()` on `result` before checking `errors[]` or `subtype`. For an envelope like `{"is_error":true,"result":"request failed","errors":["quota exceeded"]}`, the helper returns `request failed`, so the new exhaustion checks at src/adapters/process_backend.rs:165-167 and src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1442-1448 miss the quota signal and still classify the failure as retryable transport noise. That leaves a real persistent-limit response inside the exact retry loop this change is meant to avoid; scan or combine all error fields instead of preferring `result`.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:351-406` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:959-1009` now turn an optional reviewer failure that makes quorum unreachable into `AppError::InsufficientPanelMembers` instead of propagating the underlying error. That drops the original `FailureClass` (for example `TransportFailure` or `Timeout`), so the engine stops retrying and fails the run immediately even when a retry could recover the reviewer. Preserve the original error when it is the event that causes the shortfall, or wrap it in a classified error instead of returning an unclassified quorum error.

`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1866-1873` uses a raw substring check for `"until 2"` to infer a persistent reset time. Any transient rate-limit message that says things like `until 2 requests complete` or `until 2 jobs finish` will be misclassified as `BackendExhausted`, which makes the backend non-retryable and can incorrectly shrink panel quorum. Replace this with a narrower ISO-date/time check instead of treating every `until 2...` phrase as exhaustion.

In `src/adapters/process_backend.rs` around line 1872, the RESET_TIME_INDICATORS entry `"until 20"` (intended to match ISO year prefixes like "until 2026-...") could false-positive on messages like "rate limit reached, until 2000 requests are processed" or "until 200 more attempts". Consider tightening to `"until 20"` followed by a digit check (e.g. matching `until 20\d\d-` for ISO dates) or using a regex, to avoid misclassifying retryable messages as BackendExhausted.

High: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6855-6867` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7115-7127` treat any quorum shortfall with `probe_exhausted_* > 0` as `BackendExhausted`. That is wrong for mixed failures. If one backend is exhausted but another optional panel member is merely transiently unavailable, these branches still return `AppError::BackendUnavailable { failure_class: Some(BackendExhausted) }`, which makes the whole stage terminal and suppresses retries for the transient failure. Only surface `BackendExhausted` when the shortfall is caused solely by exhausted members; otherwise preserve the retryable error/`InsufficientPanelMembers` path.

Medium: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:479-512` now makes `preflight_panel_members` skip `BackendExhausted` members for every panel, but prompt-review explicitly does not degrade on exhausted validators (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:168-202`, and the dispatch path says the same at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6539-6556`). This means `preflight_check` can pass for a run whose prompt-review stage will deterministically fail later, so preflight no longer matches actual stage semantics. Gate the preflight degradation logic by stage/panel type instead of applying it generically.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': Claude returned empty result with no structured_output (contract: plan_and_implement, stdout_len: 954, session_policy: ReuseIfAllowed)

details.body: `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:218`, `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:223`, and `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:354-381` only mark OpenRouter exhaustion when the response body matches the keyword list. A bare HTTP 402 Payment Required or a generic payment/billing body that misses those exact substrings is still treated as `BackendUnavailable(None)` at probe time or `TransportFailure` at invoke time, so the run retries instead of degrading. Classify status 402 itself, or at least a small generic payment-required/billing set, as `BackendExhausted`.
